rulings.Case Title: STEVEN JULIA VS. MUSA S. DAJANI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 11, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANTS MUSA DAJANI, AND MUSA S. DAJANI TRUST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The court denies the motion for summary judgment because defendants failed to maintain their burden of production. First, par. 7 of the Complaint alleges that defendants were negligent with regard to their control of the sidewalk, sidewalk area, and street. Defendants fail to show that the condition of the sidewalk/sidewalk area did not contribute to the accident. Based on the picture of the accident site provided with the reply brief, it is unclear exactly what constitutes the sidewalk area and what constitutes the street. Second, based on SF Public Works Code sec. 400 and the factors enunciated in Rowland v. Christian, (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108, 113, (the degree of certainty that the plaintiff suffered injury, the closeness of the connection between the defendant's conduct and the injury suffered, the moral blame attached to the defendant's conduct, the policy of preventing future harm, the extent of the burden to the defendant and consequences to the community of imposing a duty to exercise care with resulting liability for breach, and the availability, cost, and prevalence of insurance for the risk involved), defendants owed a duty to repair the pothole. SF Public Works sec. 400 expressly provides that defendants owe a duty to maintain the right-of-way. That duty exists regardless of notice of the danger from the Public Works Director. The danger in this case was patent and injury to persons using the property was foreseeable. It was defendants' responsibility to maintain the accident area in a safe condition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JAN TOLU VS. THE CELLAR, A BUSINESS ENTITY OF UNKNOWN FORM DOIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday June 11, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JAN TOLU'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Dennis Hyde, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties are to be prepared to advise the Court in detail on what steps have been taken to obtain signed orders ruling on Plaintiff's motion to amend the complaint and continuing the trial date to December 7, 2015 with the discovery cutoff extended; and to provide a copy of the amended complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to hydelaw@pacbell.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSHUA TABAK VS. MULESOFT,INC. A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 11, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT TRINET GROUP, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. Granted. Defendant establishes there is a valid and enforceable written agreement that mandates arbitration of Plaintiff's claims. (Declaration of Heather Jo Donnelly, Ex. A, Terms and Conditions Agreement, par. 9.) The arbitration agreement is governed by the Federal Arbitration Act because the complaint admits that Plaintiff engaged in interstate commerce while employed by Defendant and because the agreement specifically invokes the FAA. (Id. at par. 9a; Complaint, par. 7.) Accordingly, Lab. Code sec. 229 is preempted. (Perry v. Thomas (1987) 482 U.S. 483, 492.) This litigation is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HELENA HUOH VS. ARIEL BENTOLILA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendants Raskin Real Estate Inc. And Legal One Realty, Inc.'S Motion To Consolidate Actions Of Case(S) Cud-14-650239 With Case Cud-13-646863
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 11, 2015, Line 11, Cross-Defendants Raskin Real Estate Inc. and Legal One Realty, Inc.'s Motion To Consolidate Actions Of Case CUD-14-650239 with Case CUD-13-646863. The motion to consolidate is granted. Consolidation is appropriate where separate lawsuits involve common questions of law or fact and are pending in the same court. [See CCP § 1048.] To grant or deny a motion to consolidate rests in the sound discretion of the trial court [Fellner v. Steinbaum (1955) 132 CA2d 509, 511.] Here, there is no trial set in either case, therefore this motion for consolidation is timely. Further, because the allegations in both actions involve common questions of law and fact, and because consolidation will not add any new allegations, consolidation will not prejudice Defendants and Cross Complainants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
